title: Why GM's memory chip guidance could help stabilize stocks like Micron and Sandisk
excerpt: Memory chip shortages continue to be a problem for most companies.
content_html:
Perhaps those worried about the sell-offs in momentum memory chip stocks such as Micron (MU) and Sandisk (SNDK) should read the second quarter earnings release from auto giant General Motors (GM). 

Because it sure looks like the memory chip supply shortage driving up prices is alive and well, despite market fears of a slowdown. 

Quick insight: GM posted better-than-expected second quarter earnings on Tuesday before the opening bell on Wall Street. For the second time this year, GM lifted its full-year profit forecast despite higher memory chip costs and tariff impacts. 

But GM's guidance for $1.5 billion to $2 billion commodity inflation and higher DRAM (aka memory chips) costs was initially lost in the headlines. This range from three months ago was reiterated, but it remains higher than the $1 billion to $1.5 billion guidance coming into 2026.

What's behind the move: The memory chip market is tightening as demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and advanced DRAM used in AI servers continues to outpace supply.

Companies such as SK Hynix (SKHY), Samsung Electronics (005930.KS), and Micron have largely sold out their premium AI memory capacity through much of 2026 as customers, including Nvidia (NVDA), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Meta (META), race to build AI infrastructure.

The shortage has pushed memory prices sharply higher and given suppliers greater pricing power after several years of weak industry conditions. Experts expect memory supply to remain constrained into 2027, creating a favorable backdrop for the industry's largest producers.

That backdrop has powered the share price of Micron to a record high in late June. But the stock has dropped 25% from the highs on concerns that AI demand has begun to slow, creating fears of future overcapacity for memory chip players.

The sell-off has extended to traditional semiconductor names such as Broadcom, too, with its stock down 8% in the past month per Yahoo Finance AlphaSpace data. The pullback has brought Broadcom's forward price-to-earnings multiple down to 19.8 times, below the three-year average of 29 times, according to AlphaSpace.

The bottom line: GM has been able to offset higher DRAM costs through cost cuts and better pricing on full-size trucks. As long as it can continue to do this, it should be well placed to deliver on its upwardly revised full-year guidance.

As for memory chip stocks, GM just reinforced the reason investors were bullish in the first place.

Brian Sozzi is Yahoo Finance's Executive Editor, host of the 'Power Players With Brian Sozzi' podcast and a member of Yahoo Finance's editorial leadership team. Follow Sozzi on X @BrianSozzi, Instagram, and LinkedIn. Tips on stories? Email brian.sozzi@yahoofinance.com.

content_html_zh:
也许那些担心美光 (MU) 和闪迪 (SNDK) 等动量存储芯片股遭到抛售的人应该阅读汽车巨头通用汽车 (GM) 发布的第二季度财报。

因为尽管市场担心经济放缓，但看起来存储芯片供应短缺推高价格的情况确实存在。

快速洞察：通用汽车周二在华尔街开盘前发布了好于预期的第二季度收益。 尽管存储芯片成本上升和关税影响，通用汽车今年第二次提高了全年利润预期。

但通用汽车对 15 亿至 20 亿美元商品通胀和更高 DRAM（又名内存芯片）成本的指导最初并未出现在头条新闻中。 三个月前的这一范围得到了重申，但仍高于 2026 年 10 亿至 15 亿美元的指导值。

此举背后的原因：随着人工智能服务器中使用的高带宽内存 (HBM) 和先进 DRAM 的需求持续超过供应，内存芯片市场正在趋紧。

随着英伟达 (NVDA)、微软 (MSFT)、亚马逊 (AMZN) 和 Meta (META) 等客户竞相构建人工智能基础设施，SK 海力士 (SKHY)、三星电子 (005930.KS) 和美光等公司在 2026 年的大部分时间里已基本售空其优质人工智能内存容量。

短缺导致内存价格大幅上涨，并在经历了几年疲软的行业状况后赋予供应商更大的定价权。 专家预计，到 2027 年，内存供应仍将受到限制，这为业内最大的生产商创造了有利的背景。

在这种背景下，美光科技的股价在 6 月底创下了历史新高。 但由于担心人工智能需求开始放缓，该股已从高点下跌了 25%，引发了人们对存储芯片厂商未来产能过剩的担忧。

抛售也蔓延到了 Broadcom 等传统半导体公司，根据雅虎财经 AlphaSpace 的数据，该公司股价在过去一个月下跌了 8%。 据 AlphaSpace 称，此次回调导致博通的预期市盈率降至 19.8 倍，低于 29 倍的三年平均水平。

底线：通用汽车已经能够通过成本削减和全尺寸卡车更好的定价来抵消更高的 DRAM 成本。 只要它能够继续这样做，它就应该能够很好地实现上调后的全年指引。

至于存储芯片股票，通用汽车只是强化了投资者最初看涨的理由。

Brian Sozzi 是雅虎财经的执行编辑、“Power Players With Brian Sozzi”播客的主持人，也是雅虎财经编辑领导团队的成员。 在 X @BrianSozzi、Instagram 和 LinkedIn 上关注 Sozzi。 关于故事的提示？ 电子邮件 brian.sozzi@yahoofinance.com。
